steps.title: Identify the structural requirement
steps.content: The sentence contains an independent subject (“Sarah McAnulty”) followed by a prepositional phrase and participial modifier (“as a biologist at the University of Connecticut, studying squids”). The blank sits between the subject and these modifying phrases.

steps.title: Apply the main verb requirement
steps.content:
A sentence requires a finite verb (a verb that carries tense) as its main verb. “Was working” (choice B) is a finite verb in the past tense that matches “In 2021” and completes the independent clause.

Working is a participle that needs a helping verb, while to work and to have worked are infinitives that cannot serve as main verbs.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
